nameMeanings.name: Perrot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gilles, Veronique.
French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perrotte .
Breton: from a French-influenced pet form (see 1 above) of the personal name Per, Breton form of Peter . Compare Perro .

variants.name: Pierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Antoine, Yves, Michel, Jean Claude, Mireille, Jean Robert, Francois, Monique, Raoul, Serge.
French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian:

from the personal name Pierre, French form of Peter (see also below). This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Jean and Joseph ) and in Saint Martin. It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in Trinidad and Tobago, Saint Lucia, Grenada, and Bahamas).

variants.name: Perrotte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Damien, Henri.
Altered form of French Perrot or Perrault , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.
French: from the feminine form of   Perrot  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.
